source_paper_title: On Time-Consistent Equilibrium Stopping Under Aggregation of Diverse Discount Rates

As a sharp contrast, in our framework with a general attitude function \(\varphi(x)\), it is an appealing problem to find a general characterization of the optimal equilibrium if it exists, which will be left for our future research.
title: Characterize the optimal equilibrium stopping region under general aggregation preferences
background_latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) support a one-dimensional time-homogeneous diffusion \(X=(X_t)_{t\ge 0}\) with continuous paths and the strong Markov property, started from \(X_0=x\in\mathbb R\). Let \(\mathbb F=(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0}\) be the filtration generated by \(X\), and let \(\mathcal T\) be the set of all \(\mathbb F\)-stopping times.

Let \(\rho\) be a nonnegative random discount rate with distribution function \(F_\rho\) on \([0,\infty)\), independent of \(X\). Let \(g:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a measurable payoff function. Let \(\varphi:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a strictly increasing “attitude” (aggregation) function.

For a Borel set \(R\subseteq\mathbb R\) interpreted as a stopping region, define the hitting time (given current state \(x\))
\[\tau(x,R):=\inf\{t>0: X_t^x\in R\},\]
where \(X^x\) denotes the process started at \(x\).

Define the aggregated continuation payoff under policy \(R\) by
\[J(x,R):=\int_0^\infty \varphi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau(x,R)}\, g(X^x_{\tau(x,R)})\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r).\]
Define the induced value when today’s self can either stop immediately or continue assuming future selves follow \(R\):
\[V(x,R):=\varphi(g(x))\vee J(x,R).\]

A (mild) equilibrium is a Borel set \(R\) such that it is a fixed point of the policy-improvement operator \(\Theta\) constructed from the comparison between \(\varphi(g(x))\) and \(J(x,R)\) (equivalently, a time-consistent stopping region in the sense of consistent planning). Let \(\mathcal E\) denote the collection of all such equilibria.

An optimal equilibrium is an equilibrium \(R^*\in\mathcal E\) such that \(V(x,R^*)\ge V(x,R)\) for all \(x\) and all \(R\in\mathcal E\).
problem_definition_latex:
Find general conditions and/or an explicit characterization of an optimal equilibrium \(R^*\in\mathcal E\) (when it exists) for the time-inconsistent aggregated optimal stopping problem with payoff aggregator \(J\) above, i.e., determine \(R^*\in\mathcal E\) such that
\[V(x,R^*)\ge V(x,R)\quad\text{for all }x\in\mathbb R\text{ and all }R\in\mathcal E,\]
without restricting \(\varphi\) to satisfy the structural condition that \(x\mapsto \varphi'(x)x\) is increasing (and beyond the special cases analyzed in the paper).
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb P)\) support a one-dimensional time-homogeneous diffusion \(X=(X_t)_{t\ge 0}\) with continuous paths and the strong Markov property, started from \(X_0=x\in\mathbb R\). Let \(\mathbb F=(\mathcal F_t)_{t\ge 0}\) be the filtration generated by \(X\), and let \(\mathcal T\) be the set of all \(\mathbb F\)-stopping times.

Let \(\rho\) be a nonnegative random discount rate with distribution function \(F_\rho\) on \([0,\infty)\), independent of \(X\). Let \(g:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a measurable payoff function. Let \(\varphi:\mathbb R\to\mathbb R\) be a strictly increasing “attitude” (aggregation) function.

For a Borel set \(R\subseteq\mathbb R\) interpreted as a stopping region, define the hitting time (given current state \(x\))
\[\tau(x,R):=\inf\{t>0: X_t^x\in R\},\]
where \(X^x\) denotes the process started at \(x\).

Define the aggregated continuation payoff under policy \(R\) by
\[J(x,R):=\int_0^\infty \varphi\Big(\mathbb E\big[e^{-r\tau(x,R)}\, g(X^x_{\tau(x,R)})\big]\Big)\,dF_\rho(r).\]
Define the induced value when today’s self can either stop immediately or continue assuming future selves follow \(R\):
\[V(x,R):=\varphi(g(x))\vee J(x,R).\]

A (mild) equilibrium is a Borel set \(R\) such that it is a fixed point of the policy-improvement operator \(\Theta\) constructed from the comparison between \(\varphi(g(x))\) and \(J(x,R)\) (equivalently, a time-consistent stopping region in the sense of consistent planning). An optimal equilibrium is an equilibrium \(R^*\in\mathcal E\) such that \(V(x,R^*)\ge V(x,R)\) for all \(x\) and all \(R\in\mathcal E\).

Find general conditions and/or an explicit characterization of an optimal equilibrium \(R^*\in\mathcal E\) (when it exists) for the time-inconsistent aggregated optimal stopping problem with payoff aggregator \(J\) above, i.e., determine \(R^*\in\mathcal E\) such that
\[V(x,R^*)\ge V(x,R)\quad\text{for all }x\in\mathbb R\text{ and all }R\in\mathcal E,\]
without restricting \(\varphi\) to satisfy the structural condition that \(x\mapsto \varphi'(x)x\) is increasing (and beyond the special cases analyzed in the paper).
location_in_paper: Page 28 (PDF page label 28/38), Section 5.1 "Geometric Brownian motion", Remark 5.3 (end of remark).
open_problem_quote: As a sharp contrast, in our framework with a general attitude function \(\varphi(x)\), it is an appealing problem to find a general characterization of the optimal equilibrium if it exists, which will be left for our future research.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The paper shows by explicit parametric examples that an optimal equilibrium may fail to exist or may differ from the smallest equilibrium, suggesting rich behavior. Automated searches over parametric families (choices of \(\varphi\), distributions of \(\rho\), and simple diffusions like GBM/Bessel) can test existence/optimality numerically, but the space of admissible \(\varphi\) is infinite-dimensional, so brute-force counterexample search is only feasible within restricted parametric classes.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate characterizations (e.g., conjectured barrier forms, selection principles, or sufficient conditions) can be encoded and scored by computing \(V(x,R)\) across a grid of states and equilibria for computable models (GBM/Bessel) and parametric \(\varphi\), \(F_\rho\). An evolutionary loop can search over parametric aggregators and distributions to discover patterns, propose conjectures (e.g., when optimal equilibria coincide with minimal equilibria), and generate hard instances where no global maximizer exists.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
